main_claim: BTC, local blockage and connected-pore degradation must be interpreted as distinct packed-bed responses.
boundary: Framework figure organizes existing evidence; it does not add a new DEM result.
next_upgrade: Keep the framework tied to the completed repeat-seed uncertainty and current bounded hydraulic checks.

main_claim: The baseline DEM-derived pore reconstruction is highly connected and provides a structural reference independent of outlet breakthrough.
boundary: DEM-derived pore reconstruction is numerical post-processing, not experimental CT.
next_upgrade: Add voxel-size sensitivity or pressure-flow evidence if a journal asks for structural robustness beyond the existing supplement.

main_claim: Increasing drag-to-weight ratio shifts debris downstream and enables weak breakthrough without measurable connected-pore loss.
boundary: One-way Stokes drag is a physical driving scale, not resolved pore-scale gas redistribution.
next_upgrade: A stronger upgrade would require resolved pore-scale gas redistribution or coupled flow-particle simulations.

main_claim: Increasing injected debris inventory raises local sub-voxel blockage but remains in a pressure-free pre-clogging regime in the current window.
boundary: The pressure-free Ib and pressure proxies are not pressure-calibrated safety limits.
next_upgrade: Replace the bounded cropped-domain OpenFOAM checks with validation-grade pressure-flow or experimental pressure-drop data before making safety-limit claims.

main_claim: The paper is benchmarked by separating within-study quantitative evidence, semi-quantitative mechanism comparison and bounded pressure proxies.
boundary: Benchmarking is positioning and crosswalk evidence, not experimental or CFD validation.
next_upgrade: Upgrade journal tier by adding repeat uncertainty and pressure-flow validation.

main_claim: A high-inventory localized release forms a near-outlet sparse front that slows without outlet crossing while cross-case mechanism axes show that rare-front formation is separated from bulk source release.
boundary: The 908 target-time high-inventory case, tail-lag mining and deposition-heterogeneity mining support near-outlet sparse-front formation, tail-bulk separation and relaxation from localized filtration to axial spreading; use them as bounded mechanism evidence, not a completed BTC, source-position law or critical-clogging result.
next_upgrade: Upgrade by extending localized-source cases to outlet crossing or by fitting a controlled source-position/source-inventory residence-time law.
